ddls:
CREATE TABLE "counties" (
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each county */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the county */,
  "geoid" INTEGER /* Geoid of the county */,
  "label" TEXT /* Label of the county */,
  "staetfp" INTEGER /* State FIPS code */,
  "countyfp" INTEGER /* County FIPS code */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the county */,
  "website_url" TEXT /* Website URL of the county */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email of the county */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("county_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "county_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the county where the data was collected */,
  "dataset_type" TEXT /* Type of dataset (e.g., positive cases, deaths) */,
  "dataset_category" TEXT /* Category of dataset (e.g., COVID-19, flu) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the dataset */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "date_data_last_updated" TEXT /* Date the data was last updated */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the dataset was created */,
  "last_update_date" TEXT /* Date the dataset was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_datasets_county_id FOREIGN KEY ("county_id") REFERENCES counties ("county_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "positive_cases" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each case */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the case belongs to */,
  "county_pos_cases" INTEGER /* Number of positive cases in the county */,
  "county_pos_cases_yesterday" INTEGER /* Number of positive cases in the county yesterday */,
  "county_pos_cases_change" INTEGER /* Change in positive cases in the county */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "data_collection_date" TEXT /* Date the data was collected */,
  "data_notes" TEXT /* Additional information about the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_positive_cases_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "deaths" (
  "death_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each death */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the death belongs to */,
  "county_deaths" INTEGER /* Number of deaths in the county */,
  "county_deaths_yesterday" INTEGER /* Number of deaths in the county yesterday */,
  "county_deaths_change" INTEGER /* Change in deaths in the county */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "data_collection_date" TEXT /* Date the data was collected */,
  "data_notes" TEXT /* Additional information about the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("death_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_deaths_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hospitalizations" (
  "hospitalization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hospitalization */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the hospitalization belongs to */,
  "state_number_hospitalizations" INTEGER /* Number of hospitalizations in the state */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "data_collection_date" TEXT /* Date the data was collected */,
  "data_notes" TEXT /* Additional information about the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hospitalization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hospitalizations_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tests" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the test belongs to */,
  "state_number_tested" INTEGER /* Number of tests conducted in the state */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "data_collection_date" TEXT /* Date the data was collected */,
  "data_notes" TEXT /* Additional information about the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tests_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "outbreaks" (
  "outbreak_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each outbreak */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the outbreak belongs to */,
  "state_number_of_outbreaks" INTEGER /* Number of outbreaks in the state */,
  "outbreak_type" TEXT /* Type of outbreak (e.g., COVID-19, flu) */,
  "outbreak_location" TEXT /* Location of the outbreak (e.g., school, workplace) */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "data_collection_date" TEXT /* Date the data was collected */,
  "data_notes" TEXT /* Additional information about the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("outbreak_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_outbreaks_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization of the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the dataset */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the dataset was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method of access (e.g., web, API) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location of access (e.g., IP address, city) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
